description:
IMAGE 1
Score: 8.8/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Main headline ("Writing Practice Books For Kids") is too generic and doesn't communicate the key value proposition or age group instantly.


Book stack overlaps heavily, making individual covers and bundle contents slightly difficult to distinguish on mobile.



IMAGE 2
Score: 7.6/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Too much text spread across the image, making it difficult to scan on mobile devices.


Weak visual hierarchy; benefits are separated from the corresponding books, forcing buyers to spend extra time understanding the layout.



IMAGE 3
Score: 8.2/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Product page occupies a relatively small portion of the canvas, reducing visibility of important tracing details.


Headline uses too many font styles and colors, weakening readability and premium appearance.



IMAGE 4
Score: 8.4/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Feature text is fragmented into multiple short headings instead of one strong customer-focused benefit.


Book spread could be zoomed in further to better showcase tracing quality and page details.



IMAGE 5
Score: 8.4/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Important features compete equally for attention instead of emphasizing one primary selling point.


Upper text block occupies significant space while the educational content remains relatively small.



IMAGE 6
Score: 8.3/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Activity pages are detailed but appear too small for mobile viewing.


Headline lacks a stronger emotional or benefit-driven message that immediately attracts parents.



IMAGE 7
Score: 8.1/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Large colored graphic dominates the image while the instructional text remains lengthy and difficult to skim.


Sequence explanation could be simplified into concise icons or shorter benefit statements for faster comprehension.



IMAGE 8
Score: 8.9/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Product books are absent, reducing immediate association between the lifestyle image and the actual product.


Headline could better communicate a tangible product benefit rather than a broad emotional statement.



IMAGE 9
Score: 6.8/10
Reasons for the deducted marks:


Functions more as a company introduction than a product-selling image, offering little purchase-driving information.


Excessive paragraph text is unlikely to be read on Amazon mobile devices and does not effectively communicate product benefits.
